steps.title: Identify the three-item series
steps.content:
The sentence lists three pieces of data being shown: length (62 km), slip rate (between 0.2 and 1.0 mm/yr), and most recent deformation (less than 15 thousand years ago).

In Standard English, a three-item series requires proper punctuation to separate items and parenthetical details must attach directly to their nouns.

steps.title: Apply the correct series punctuation
steps.content:
Choice C correctly keeps parenthetical details attached directly to their nouns—length (62 km), slip rate (between 0.2 and 1.0 mm/yr)—then uses commas to separate all three series items before and most recent deformation.

Choices A, B, and D all violate series punctuation. Choice A inserts extraneous commas between each noun and its parenthetical detail and uses semicolons in place of commas to separate series items. Choice B inserts extraneous commas between each noun and its parenthetical. Choice D omits all punctuation between series items entirely.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
